assertions.expectation: Identifies this as an admission webhook compatibility issue — the webhook is rejecting pod creation requests
assertions.reason: The response correctly identifies this as a cert-manager admission webhook compatibility issue with GKE 1.30 and explains that the webhook is blocking pod creation requests

assertions.expectation: Provides commands to identify the problematic webhook (kubectl get validatingwebhookconfigurations / mutatingwebhookconfigurations)
assertions.reason: The response includes specific commands: 'kubectl get validatingwebhookconfigurations | grep cert-manager' and 'kubectl get mutatingwebhookconfigurations | grep cert-manager'

assertions.expectation: Offers an immediate fix: temporarily disable or remove the problematic webhook
assertions.reason: The response provides Option A which involves temporarily disabling/deleting the webhook as an immediate fix

assertions.expectation: Provides the kubectl delete command for the webhook configuration as emergency mitigation
assertions.reason: The response includes the specific command: 'kubectl delete validatingwebhookconfigurations cert-manager-webhook'

assertions.expectation: Warns that deleting the webhook removes validation — it's a temporary fix
assertions.reason: The response explicitly states 'You'll lose certificate validation temporarily but existing certs continue working' and emphasizes this is a temporary solution

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking cert-manager version compatibility with Kubernetes 1.30
assertions.reason: The response includes commands to check cert-manager version and explicitly discusses upgrading to a version compatible with Kubernetes 1.30

assertions.expectation: Advises upgrading cert-manager to a version that supports 1.30 as the permanent fix
assertions.reason: The response has a dedicated 'Permanent Fix' section that specifically recommends upgrading cert-manager to version v1.14.4 or latest stable for 1.30 compatibility

assertions.expectation: Mentions setting failurePolicy to Ignore as a less drastic alternative to deletion
assertions.reason: Option B provides the kubectl patch command to set failurePolicy to 'Ignore' as a safer alternative to deletion

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to verify pods can be created after the fix
assertions.reason: The response includes a 'Validate the fix' section with specific commands like 'kubectl run test-pod --image=nginx --rm -it --restart=Never -- echo "Pod creation works"'
